comments: First session broadcast of As Above...So Below and an archive session from Lone Star.

sessions.details: Recorded: 1977-02-28. First Broadcast on Alan Freeman's Rock Show on 25 June 1977 (Alan Freeman)

track_listing.track: Play It Loud (LP - Denim And Leather) Carrere
I Turn To Stone (LP - Nightflight) RCA
Shake It Lucy (LP - Lucky For Some) Warner Bros.
The Spirit Of Radio (2xLP - Exit Stage Left) Anthem
Broon's Bane (2xLP - Exit Stage Left) Anthem
The Trees (2xLP - Exit Stage Left) Anthem
Men Of War (2xLP - Double Trouble) Virgin

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Oh, hello there. This is TV Yellow Radio here, Thomas the Vance.
And welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Right, tonight we have two sessions. Out of the Archives from 1977, an excellent set by Lone Star.
Plus the first ever broadcast by a four-piece band out of St. Albans who play a softer sort of rock.
They're called As Above, So Below.
The new releases by Rush from their new live double album by Budgie, Climax Blues Band, The Enid and Dillon.
Some classic cuts are on offer from Judas Priest and others in the Friday Night Connection, plus of course the answer to last week's connection as well, and a new one.
But tonight, we start with this.
Now that of course is Saxon, as opposed to it couldn't be anybody else with that sound.
Here's a track from their album which is kicking around the charts at the moment called Denim and Leather, the track you heard that was called Playin' Loud.
